agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review confirmed transactional emails cannot be opted out of. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird's v2. The feature is scoped to a single PATCH endpoint for toggling marketing emails, with a hard block on disabling transactional emails.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected with HTTP 403 and error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be freely toggled by the user","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false and the change persists"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences","implementation_note":"Accept a JSON body with optional boolean fields. Currently only marketing_emails_enabled is user-mutable."},{"decision":"Store marketing opt-out as a boolean field: marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value should be true (opted in). Field lives on the user preferences model."},{"decision":"Reject transactional_emails_enabled=false with 403","implementation_note":"If the request body includes transactional_emails_enabled: false, return 403 with body {\"error_code\": \"TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED\"}. Do NOT silently ignore the field — fail explicitly."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back into marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Disabling transactional emails is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; no preferences are changed"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated emails such as password resets and security alerts. Cannot be disabled by users (legal requirement)."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User-togglable."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule said transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Bird v2 removed AC2 entirely after legal review.","resolution":"Bird v2 supersedes v1. Transactional email opt-out is not permitted. v1's internal contradiction is moot — follow v2 exclusively."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Marketing email toggle behavior","403 rejection for transactional opt-out","Endpoint shape and field naming","Domain rules — legal basis is clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value of marketing_emails_enabled not explicitly stated by Bird or MJ (assumed true)","Whether PATCH should reject the entire request or only the transactional field if both fields are sent together"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 is authoritative and fully supersedes v1","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true (opted in)","If a PATCH request contains both a valid marketing change and an invalid transactional change, the entire request is rejected (fail-closed)"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for consistency before producing the handoff brief.

Bird has two submissions - v1 and v2. v2 is the latest and supersedes v1. Key changes: AC2 (transactional opt-out) was removed, and AC3 was added (403 on attempt to disable transactional).

MJ's submission aligns with Bird's v2:
- Marketing opt-out via boolean field
- 403 on attempt to disable transactional emails
- PATCH endpoint

Classification: `none` — all outputs are consistent. Bird v2 and MJ agree. Bird v1 is superseded by v2.


Now I'm putting together the handoff brief summarizing the aligned requirements and decisions.
